rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment Against Defendant Williams And Burrows, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Specially appearing Industrial Indemnity Company and United States Fire Insurance Company's motion to set aside the default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc. is granted. The default judgment entered against Williams & Burrows, Inc. on December 10, 2015 is set aside solely as related to Industrial Indemnity Company and United States Fire Insurance Company. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2019 in Department 503, Line 6. Specially appearing Industrial Indemnity Company and United States Fire Insurance Company's motion to set aside the default judgment entered against defendant Williams & Burrows, Inc.: In view of the C.C.P. 170.6 peremptory challenge to the Honorable Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, exercised on July 6, 2015, the Presiding Judge is assigning this motion to the Honorable Teri L. Jackson for hearing in Department 613 on March 11, 2019 at 9:45 am. Hearing Required. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of the reporter. = (503/GLW)
